 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-62aa3","title":"[REVIEW-A9][P2] connectors/zapier/src/client.rs:218: action_id path injection reaches Zapier execute endpoint","description":"ZapierClient::execute_action interpolates external action_id directly into /exposed/{action_id}/execute/. The action_id comes from invoke input via require_str, so slashes, query delimiters, fragments, or dot segments can alter the API path on the configured host. Percent-encode or validate action_id as one path segment and add a regression test that adversarial action_id cannot change the endpoint path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:44:06.102767Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:57:04.438878Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:57:04.438346Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2919,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62aa3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fixed in commit c82e685c (cc_2/SunnyMoose). New validate_action_id helper enforces non-empty + ≤128 bytes + ASCII alphanumeric + dash + underscore allow-set; execute_action calls it before format!. 8 regression tests cover path separator (id/../admin), dot segment (foo.bar), query/fragment (?#&), percent-encoded (%2F %2e%2e), empty/whitespace, oversized, non-ASCII. Rejection maps to FcpError::InvalidRequest 1003 via existing ZapierError::InvalidInput. 255 fcp-zapier tests passing. Same defense pattern as br-898o4: validate at boundary, not surface as status.","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:57:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","title":"[MOR/C3.4] Make capability constraints mandatory; reject null/empty constraints","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:57.050666Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T23:50:57.007185Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T23:50:57.006485Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P0-critical","capability","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-800e3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:41.067762Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8whb3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:06.752983Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ky3yv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:09.737664Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2643,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Make capability constraints mandatory in CapabilityToken. Reject tokens with null/empty/missing constraints. Define a DENY-ALL default when constraints are absent (empty resource list = no operations allowed). WHY: H2 Adversarial found capability ceiling constraint bypass (#9, HIGH): if constraints are None, enforce_resource_constraints() is never called. An attacker-controlled issuer can issue unconstrained capabilities to arbitrary resources. J1 Deontic found this violates the default-deny principle — a null constraint should mean no access, not unlimited access. WHERE: crates/fcp-core/src/capability.rs — CapabilityToken verification path, specifically the constraint enforcement section. Change: if constraints is None, return Err(FcpError::CapabilityDenied) instead of skipping. Also update CapabilityToken construction to require constraints. ACCEPTANCE: (1) Tokens without constraints are rejected at verification time. (2) Token construction API requires constraints parameter (not Optional). (3) Empty constraint set = deny all (not allow all). (4) At least 4 tests: token with valid constraints passes, token with null constraints rejected, token with empty constraints rejected, token with overly-broad constraints still works (constraint narrowing is separate). ESTIMATED EFFORT: 1-2 days. DESIGN NOTE: This is one of the highest-impact-to-effort-ratio changes. Removing one Option<> wrapper eliminates an entire class of privilege escalation. DEPENDS ON: C3.1 (phantom tokens) — because the constraint check happens during verify(), and the phantom type ensures verify() is always called.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:36:49Z"},{"id":2648,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","author":"jemanuel","text":"CROSS-EPIC DEP: Depends on C1.3 (RevocationFreshnessClass in manifests) because both C1.3 and C3.4 add mandatory fields to the capability/manifest validation path. They should be coordinated to avoid two separate breaking changes to the validation pipeline. Implement them together in the same verification method.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:41Z"},{"id":2663,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","author":"jemanuel","text":"C3.4 mandatory constraints: CapabilityConstraints::is_empty(), verification rejects null/empty, constraints_cbor() on CwtClaims+Builder, 4 acceptance tests. Commit 45ecad49.","created_at":"2026-04-10T23:50:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-63o","title":"fcp.zendesk: Ticket CRUD Operations","description":"# fcp.zendesk: Ticket CRUD Operations (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe, typed ticket CRUD so agents can:
- create tickets
- read ticket details and thread metadata
- update status/priority/assignee/tags

Ticket content often contains PII; strict logging hygiene is required.

## Scope
- Create/get/update ticket.
- List/search tickets with filters and pagination.
- Soft-delete/archive (dangerous; optional).
- Attachments are handled as bounded downloads/uploads (may be separate surface).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `zendesk.tickets.read`
- `zendesk.tickets.write` (dangerous-ish)
- Optional: `zendesk.tickets.delete` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to the Zendesk subdomain host.
- PII hygiene:
  - do not log raw ticket bodies or requester emails
  - logs include only ticket ids and field names changed
- For writes: receipts + audit events required.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `zendesk.ticket.create`
  - `zendesk.ticket.get`
  - `zendesk.ticket.update`
  - `zendesk.ticket.search`
  - Optional: `zendesk.ticket.archive`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - CRUD flows
  - pagination
  - permission denied
  - receipts/audit for mutations
  - redaction

## Acceptance criteria
- Ticket mutations are mechanically authorized and auditable.
- No PII leaks into logs.

Operations: zendesk.create_ticket, zendesk.get_ticket, zendesk.update_ticket, zendesk.delete_ticket, zendesk.search_tickets, zendesk.list_ticket_comments. 58 tests pass (34 unit + 24 integration). Includes capability gating, introspection metadata, and error handling.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:00:47Z"}]}

The commit exposes parse_error_response through a doc-hidden __fuzz wrapper, registers the fuzz target, and exercises raw/structured/malformed error envelopes across auth, not-found, validation, conflict, rate-limit, and generic API statuses. Verified: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod3-fixed cargo check -p fcp-github; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod3-fixed cargo check --bin fuzz_github_api_error_response (from fuzz/); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod3-fixed cargo fuzz run fuzz_github_api_error_response -- -runs=1 -max_len=4096 (completed 2 runs from empty corpus); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod3-fixed cargo fmt --package fcp-github --check; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod3-fixed cargo fmt --check (from fuzz/); git diff --check; ubs on touched Rust files.","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:05:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-674lv","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X: verify_cookie / compute_cookie field-binding metamorphic fuzz","description":"fcp_protocol::session::compute_cookie + verify_cookie (session.rs:989-1026)
implement the stateless DoS-prevention cookie under HMAC-SHA256(cookie_key,
canonical CBOR of from || to || eph_pubkey || nonce || timestamp || suites
|| transport_limits). The host issues this cookie in response to an
unauthenticated hello and refuses to advance the handshake until the peer
echoes a valid one — so the cookie is the gate that closes the trivial
unauthenticated DoS surface against handshake state allocation.

Existing fuzz coverage:
  - decode_cookie_bytes (wire-format length + identity check) — fuzz_session
  - session.rs handshake decode — fuzz_session
  - session_metamorphic — verify_session_mac binding (mac_key, session_id,
    direction, seq, frame), NOT cookie

The metamorphic relations a regression would silently break are NOT
covered:

  1. **Round-trip**: verify_cookie(key, hello, compute_cookie(key, hello))
     MUST return Ok (otherwise the cookie gate is unusable).
  2. **Determinism**: compute_cookie is a pure function — same (key, hello)
     MUST yield byte-identical cookies.
  3. **Field-binding**: bit-flipping any of (from, to, eph_pubkey, nonce,
     timestamp, suites, transport_limits) MUST cause verify_cookie to
     return InvalidCookie. A regression that dropped, e.g., the timestamp
     from compute_cookie's input would let an attacker reuse a captured
     cookie indefinitely; dropping eph_pubkey would let them retrofit a
     fresh ephemeral pubkey to a captured cookie (mid-handshake
     swap-in surface).
  4. **Key-binding**: different cookie_key on identical hello MUST yield
     a cookie that fails verification under the original key.
  5. **Cookie-tamper rejection**: bit-flipping the cookie bytes
     themselves MUST cause InvalidCookie (anchors the ct_eq path at
     session.rs:1021).

Acceptance:
  - fuzz/fuzz_targets/session_cookie_binding.rs
  - 5 MRs above + once-gated regression anchor exercising the timestamp-
    binding path with a hand-constructed pair that defeats the
    "captured cookie reused with a different timestamp" attack.
  - Registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml.
  - cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:30:34.398168Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T21:32:26.415703Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T21:32:26.415221Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3044,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-674lv","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in commit d25b6659. fuzz/fuzz_targets/session_cookie_binding.rs — 5 MRs (round-trip, determinism, per-field binding ×7, key-binding, cookie-tamper rejection) + once-gated anchors for timestamp-binding (defeats indefinite replay) and eph_pubkey-binding (defeats mid-handshake ephemeral swap-in). cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean. NOTE: pivoted from session_finish (no such frame exists in fcp-protocol) to verify_cookie — same handshake-DoS-gate area, fuzz-uncovered.","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:32:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-691o","title":"[IDEA-11.3] Streaming health tests + simulated loop","description":"## Goal
Provide tests for streaming health behavior using a simulated loop.

## Details
- Simulate missed heartbeat and ensure state transitions.
- Verify log fields emitted per schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests validate deterministic health transitions.","notes":"Implemented streaming heartbeat timeout unit test in fcp-sdk runtime + existing supervisor heartbeat timeout/log test covers missed heartbeat + log fields. Gates: fmt/check/clippy green. Waiting on parent flywheel_connectors-j7w1 to close.

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-91jd2","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-25: control-plane retention_for_schema classification + namespace-match defense","description":"retention_for_schema in fcp-protocol/control_plane.rs is the NORMATIVE classifier that decides whether a control-plane object MUST be stored for audit (Required) or MAY be dropped after processing (Ephemeral). Documented invariants: (1) explicit Required prefixes (fcp.invoke, fcp.receipt, fcp.approval, fcp.secret, fcp.revoke, fcp.audit, fcp.grant, fcp.membership) win, (2) Ephemeral prefixes (fcp.health, fcp.handshake, fcp.status, fcp.introspect, fcp.configure, fcp.simulate, fcp.ping, fcp.heartbeat) classify as drop-after-processing, (3) UNKNOWN schemas default to Required (fail-safe toward auditability), (4) namespace_matches_prefix uses dot-delimited semantics so 'fcp.heartbeat-evil' MUST NOT match 'fcp.heartbeat' (pathological-namespace defense), (5) 'fcp.heartbeat.v2' DOES match (sub-namespace inheritance), (6) Required prefix checked BEFORE Ephemeral (priority ordering). Zero conformance coverage. cc_2 domain (fcp-conformance + fcp-protocol).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:10:35.321266Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T22:11:54.159055Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T22:11:54.158421Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","protocol","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3079,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-91jd2","author":"jemanuel","text":"4f008d9f adds control_plane_retention_classification_conformance.rs with 14 tests pinning retention_for_schema: 5 Required prefixes (audit-critical), 3 Ephemeral prefixes (drop-after-processing), unknown-default-Required failsafe, pathological-namespace match defense ('fcp.heartbeat-evil' MUST NOT match 'fcp.heartbeat'), legitimate sub-namespace inheritance ('fcp.heartbeat.v2' inherits Ephemeral, 'fcp.audit.v3' inherits Required), classification independent of name+version, requires_storage helper consistency, and empty-namespace default. Mid-stream Rust fix: SchemaId::new needs explicit deref when iterating &[&str]. All 14 pass in <0.01s.","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:11:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-94bok","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-auth-schema 5 tests broken by canonical CBOR ordering switch in 6df66c79","description":"crates/fcp-auth-schema/src/claims.rs — after commit 6df66c79 switched to_canonical_cbor/from_canonical_cbor to RFC 8949 byte-ordered map keys, 5 hand-rolled CBOR test inputs now fail from_canonical_cbor with Decode("non-canonical CBOR encoding") because they either (a) do not sort keys, (b) sort by i64/i128 numeric rather than RFC 8949 encoded-byte order. Failing tests: decode_invalid_timestamp_reports_actual_value_not_zero, decode_out_of_range_u16_yields_out_of_range_error_with_value, decode_out_of_range_u64_preserves_value, type_mismatch_surfaces_helpful_error, unknown_labels_are_ignored_on_decode. Repro: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline -p fcp-auth-schema -> 5 failed, 16 passed. Suggested fix: have each test build its CBOR bytes via fcp_cbor::to_canonical_cbor(&ciborium::Value::Map(entries)) (already a workspace dep of fcp-auth-schema post-6df66c79) instead of manual ciborium::into_writer. Reviewer: cc_1.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:08.360288Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:26:28.755931Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:26:28.755676Z","close_reason":"Replaced manual ciborium::into_writer(...) with fcp_cbor::to_canonical_cbor in 5 hand-rolled CBOR test fixtures + dropped the obsolete manual sort_by_key in unknown_labels_are_ignored_on_decode (fcp_cbor::to_canonical_cbor already performs RFC 8949 key ordering). Verified: all 21 fcp-auth-schema lib tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-96mui","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-38: EnforcementCheckOrder canonical 11-stage pipeline + CheckOutcome predicate contract","description":"EnforcementCheckOrder::canonical_order returns the NORMATIVE 11-check pipeline (CanonicalDecode, ZoneMembership, CapabilityVerify, HolderProof, CheckpointFreshness, RevocationFreshness, TaintApproval, PolicyCeiling, ConnectorManifest, Budget, RateLimit). The docstring explicitly says 'all FCP runtimes MUST evaluate enforcement checks in this order' — drift between implementations would silently change which check surfaces an error first, masking real failures behind incidental ones. Plus index_of must agree with canonical_order position, as_str matches snake_case wire form, runs_before is the < relation on indices, and CheckOutcome::is_allow/is_deny are mutually exclusive. cc_2 domain (fcp-conformance + fcp-core).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:06:09.561568Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T23:07:55.863899Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T23:07:55.863393Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","core","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3127,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-96mui","author":"jemanuel","text":"11f25790 adds enforcement_check_order_conformance.rs with 18 tests pinning the NORMATIVE 11-stage enforcement pipeline + CheckOutcome predicates. Full canonical sequence pinned literally, COUNT==11 invariant, index_of/canonical_order agreement, runs_before strict-less-than full 11x11 matrix + irreflexivity, as_str snake_case for all 11, Display==as_str, json serde, determinism, plus 3 design-rule orderings (cheap-before-crypto, crypto-before-stateful which prevents budget enumeration via forged tokens, freshness-before-business-logic), CheckOutcome Allow/Deny/Skip predicate triples (Skip is mutually exclusive with both — caller MUST handle explicitly), serde roundtrip for all 3 variants, 'outcome' tag rename, exactly-once variant coverage. Mid-stream HashSet not BTreeSet fix (no Ord derive). All 18 pass in <0.01s.","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:07:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","title":"[LC] Tests: unit + integration","description":"# [LC] Tests: unit + integration

## Goal
Validate rollout logic, rollback triggers, and pinning behavior.

## Unit Tests
- Canary success -> full rollout.
- Canary failure -> rollback with audit.
- Pin prevents auto-upgrade.

## Integration Tests
- Simulated connector health regression triggers rollback.

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON logs per transition (state_from, state_to, reason, duration_ms).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic logs and stable state transitions.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.
[AlienArtifactReview] Updated 2026-02-02T23:58:59.240755+00:00","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldOx","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:03:57.847574850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T07:06:11.963332Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T07:06:11.963308Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-334n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-3fdt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-s9t8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":991,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: The 48 rollout unit tests in fcp-host/src/rollout.rs already cover all required scenarios:
- Canary success -> full rollout: tests promote_success, ctrl_schedule_from_production
- Canary failure -> rollback with audit: tests eval_crash_loop, eval_error_rate, failure_streak_triggers_rollback, ctrl_unavailable_rolls_back
- Pin prevents auto-upgrade: test eval_pinned
- Structured JSON logs: evidence bundles are deterministic and serializable (evidence_roundtrip, audit_event_digest_and_roundtrip)
Additional supply_chain module adds 45 tests for verification gate (policy enforcement, caching, determinism).
Total fcp-host unit tests: 588, all pass.","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:39:42Z"},{"id":1018,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","author":"GoldOx","text":"Added rollout rollback/logging integration coverage in crates/fcp-host/tests/host_connector_integration.rs and cleaned crates/fcp-host/src/batch.rs so current fcp-host verification stays green. Verification completed with: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.target-goldox-host-cache cargo clippy -p fcp-host --all-targets -- -D warnings; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.target-goldox-host-cache cargo test -p fcp-host --all-targets; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.target-goldox-host-cache cargo check --workspace --all-targets; rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on the touched fcp-host files.","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:56:26Z"},{"id":1156,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Verified all acceptance criteria met. 126 rollout tests pass (canary success/failure, rollback audit, pin prevents auto-upgrade, structured JSON logs).  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lte5","title":"TEST: fcp-webhook Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-webhook Unit Test Suite

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for webhook handling library.

## Test Categories

### Signature Verification
- [ ] HMAC-SHA256 verification (Stripe, GitHub, etc.)
- [ ] Ed25519 signature verification
- [ ] Timestamp validation (replay attack prevention)
- [ ] Multiple signature schemes
- [ ] Invalid signature rejection

### Webhook Parsing
- [ ] JSON payload parsing
- [ ] Form-encoded payload parsing
- [ ] Content-Type handling
- [ ] Charset handling

### Event Routing
- [ ] Event type dispatch
- [ ] Wildcard subscriptions
- [ ] Event filtering

### Delivery Management
- [ ] Idempotency handling (deduplication)
- [ ] Event ordering guarantees
- [ ] Retry scheduling for failed processing
- [ ] Dead letter queue handling

### Security
- [ ] IP allowlist verification
- [ ] TLS certificate validation
- [ ] Request size limits
- [ ] Timeout enforcement

## Mocking Strategy
- Generate valid/invalid webhook payloads
- Mock signature generation with known keys
- Test various provider formats (GitHub, Stripe, Slack, etc.)

## Coverage Target: >95%","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T17:12:36.431123151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:37.210724460Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:37.210724460Z","close_reason":"Webhook functionality is connector-specific, not a platform crate. Webhook testing belongs in individual connector test suites.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lte5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-vi7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lv8h","title":"TEST: InvokeRequest/Response Unit Tests (Schema Validation, Holder Proof, Binding)","description":"# TEST: InvokeRequest/Response Unit Tests


## Goal
Prove InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse validation, binding rules, and holder proof verification are correct with golden vectors and fuzz resilience.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for the InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse types and their validation logic.

## Test Categories

### Schema Validation
- [ ] Valid InvokeRequest with all required fields
- [ ] Invalid InvokeRequest (missing operation_id) → rejection
- [ ] Invalid InvokeRequest (malformed arguments) → rejection
- [ ] Schema hash mismatch detection
- [ ] Argument type validation against operation schema

### Holder Proof Verification
- [ ] Valid holder_proof verifies correctly
- [ ] Missing holder_proof for risky operation → rejection
- [ ] Holder proof with wrong session key → rejection
- [ ] Holder proof replay detection
- [ ] Holder proof binding to request_id

### Binding Rules
- [ ] capability_token_id binding is enforced
- [ ] zone_id binding is checked
- [ ] connector_id binding is validated
- [ ] aud_binary binding for risky operations

### Response Validation
- [ ] Success response with valid result
- [ ] Error response with proper error taxonomy
- [ ] DecisionReceipt attached for denials
- [ ] Streaming response envelope handling

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/invoke/`
- `invoke_request_valid.cbor`
- `invoke_request_invalid_schema.cbor`
- `invoke_response_success.cbor`
- `invoke_response_denial.cbor`

## Logging Requirements
All tests emit structured JSON logs:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_invoke_schema_validation",
  "phase": "setup|execute|verify",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "operation_id": "...",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "evidence": {...}
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All InvokeRequest/Response validations are covered
- Golden vectors exist and match
- No panics on malformed inputs
- Fuzz target for request parsing exists

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-m3p2","title":"[IDEA-03.2] SDK SequentialEventProcessor utility","description":"## Goal
Provide a reusable SDK helper for per-key sequential processing with bounded queues.

## Details
- Implement `SequentialEventProcessor` keyed by `stream_key`.
- Bounded queue per key + global cap to prevent unbounded memory.
- Configurable drop/timeout policy for slow consumers.

## Tests
- Unit tests for ordering within a key and fairness across keys.
- Stress test to ensure queue caps enforced.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Connectors can use the utility without custom queueing logic.
- Ordering within a key is preserved deterministically.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-nvt","title":"fcp.sentry: Issue assignment and status management","description":"# fcp.sentry: Issue assignment and status management (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow safe, auditable manipulation of Sentry issue workflow state:
- assign/unassign
- resolve/unresolve
- ignore/unignore
- set priority (if supported)

These are write operations that can affect on-call workflows.

## Scope
### Write operations
- `sentry.issue.assign`
- `sentry.issue.resolve`
- `sentry.issue.ignore`
- `sentry.issue.update_priority`

### Read support
- fetch minimal issue metadata required for optimistic concurrency (if available).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.issues.write`

Policy guidance:
- Some orgs may require approvals for resolving/ignoring issues.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For every write:
  - emit OperationReceipt + AuditEvent
  - include issue id + action + correlation id
  - never include raw issue payloads in logs

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.issue.assign`
  - `sentry.issue.set_status` (resolve/ignore/unresolve)
  - `sentry.issue.set_priority`
- Denial reason codes for policy engine.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - capability gating
  - approval gating (when policy requires)
  - error mapping (permission denied, conflict)
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Issue workflow actions are safe, explainable, and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","title":"[FCP3/P4] Converge host and SDK on one execution model","description":"Background: once kernel, policy, evidence, and durable semantics exist as real owners, fcp-host and fcp-sdk must converge on one execution model. The host should orchestrate and supervise; the SDK should express the same lifecycle and semantics for connector authors; neither should redefine the platform.

Scope: refactor host into a consumer of those lower layers, publish a neutral SDK app contract, and adapt at least one connector family end to end. The phase must cover lifecycle, checkpoint, drain, health, resume, and error semantics with crate local unit tests, cross crate integration coverage, connector author ergonomics checks, and scripted end to end transcripts that include detailed structured diagnostics for both operator and connector author workflows. This phase is complete when host and SDK describe the same runtime, checkpoint, drain, health, and error semantics rather than parallel interpretations.

Acceptance: connector authors and host operators can rely on one execution vocabulary, one set of diagnostics, and reproducible test and transcript artifacts that prove the shared model in practice rather than only in architecture prose.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:01.782511Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T19:02:53.482981Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T19:02:53.482824Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","host","mesh-first","phase-4","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.291708Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1fu5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.091610Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.495939Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.706332Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:12.898042Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2051,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Host convergence is the point where the platform stops being conceptually centered on fcp-host. Add a structure-aware fuzz target for Amplitude typed response/error deserializers so untrusted JSON response bodies exercise ChartQueryResponse, CohortsListResponse, EventsExportResponse, Cohort, ExportedEvent, and ApiErrorResponse without going through HTTP.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:06:52.460222Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T23:06:56.393371Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["amplitude","fwc","tests"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-o1na6","title":"[PERF] fcp-bootstrap::open_authenticated_session: global PKCS#11 lock serializes all sessions","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:451-503 and 585-597. open_authenticated_session() grabs provider_probe_lock() before PKCS#11 initialize/open/login and keeps that MutexGuard alive inside the session close closure until the caller eventually closes the authenticated session. Enumeration sessions do the same for their whole lifetime. That turns hardware-token rotation, cert enumeration, and key enumeration into a process-wide serialized critical section: one long-lived session blocks unrelated providers/slots from even starting, so throughput collapses under parallel bootstrap or rotation work. Proposed improvement: narrow the lock to the init/finalize transition or make it provider-scoped/refcounted instead of holding one global mutex for the full lifetime of every authenticated or enumeration session.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:28.975339Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:46:18.096552Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:46:18.096214Z","close_reason":"PKCS#11 session opens now use provider-scoped active-session accounting so init/finalize stay serialized without holding the global lock for the full authenticated session lifetime","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-o41gx","title":"TEST-FUZZ-CBOR-FLOAT: float canonicalization rules under arbitrary bit patterns","description":"Add fuzz target for fcp-cbor float canonicalization (lib.rs:492-499).

Rules:
(a) NaN any-bit-pattern -> SerializationError::NonFiniteFloat
(b) +/-Infinity -> NonFiniteFloat
(c) -0.0 -> normalized to 0.0 in encoded bytes
(d) finite values round-trip via to_canonical_cbor -> from_reader

Existing fuzz_canonicalize_map_deterministic explicitly skips NaN (the
arbitrary_leaf branch substitutes 0.0 when is_nan), so the rejection
branch is uncovered.

Properties: (1) panic-free over arbitrary u64 reinterpreted as f64;
(2) NaN/Inf rejection over signaling/quiet NaN payloads + +-Infinity
bit patterns; (3) -0.0 canonical zero - encode(-0.0) byte-equals
encode(0.0); (4) finite-value round-trip stability.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:02:24.357653Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T21:06:49.145428Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T21:06:49.144906Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3026,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o41gx","author":"jemanuel","text":"Added fuzz_cbor_float_canonical. 5 properties: (1) to_canonical_cbor::<f64> total over arbitrary 64-bit patterns; (2) NaN any-payload→NonFiniteFloat; (3) ±Inf→NonFiniteFloat; (4) -0.0 normalization — encode(-0.0) byte-equals encode(0.0) so content-addressing assigns same ObjectId; (5) finite encode→decode→encode round-trip stability. Once-gated regression anchor: -0.0 canonicalization, NaN rejection, +Inf rejection, with f64 bit-pattern sanity checks. Existing canonicalize_map_deterministic explicitly skips NaN, leaving the rejection branch unfuzzed. Compile + clippy + rustfmt clean via rch with local fallback.","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:06:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-o6jc1","title":"[REVIEW R2 P4][MED] fcp-e2e/homeassistant: harness handshake only seeds state and fabricates response","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/tests/homeassistant_compliance_e2e.rs:65-93 calls handle_handshake(json!({"session_id": ...})) only to seed connector state, then constructs the HandshakeResponse locally with the requested capabilities and a hardcoded manifest hash. That masks real connector handshake behavior and cannot catch response-schema or capability-negotiation drift. Fix: route the real HandshakeRequest through the connector and assert the connector-returned handshake contract directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:51:20.700384Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T20:12:15.512224Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T20:12:15.511967Z","close_reason":"Implemented real handshake wiring and response-contract assertions in homeassistant e2e harness","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-znneb","title":"TEST-AUDIT: empty chain with head fails append-only verification","description":"security-audit-for-saas fallback from cod_1. fcp-audit::verify_chain currently reports Warn for an empty entry list when a ChainHead is supplied, and audit.chain.empty is marked non-critical. A verifier that receives a head but no history cannot prove append-only continuity or the referenced tip, so the path should fail closed and have regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:30:45.845071Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T21:43:45.442997Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T21:43:45.442512Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["security","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3053,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-znneb","author":"jemanuel","text":"22514e83 ships TEST-AUDIT: empty chain with head fails append-only verification; verified with env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod1-fixed rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-audit verify_chain_empty_with_head (1 passed) and env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod1-fixed rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-audit verify_issue_is_critical (3 passed).","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:43:44Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-zp0q4","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X: reject ambiguous WhatsApp signature headers","description":"testing-fuzzing/security pass on the WhatsApp webhook auth parser. verify_and_parse accepts forwarded headers as a HashMap and header_value_case_insensitive returns the first case-insensitive X-Hub-Signature-256 match. Payloads containing duplicate logical signature headers with different casing can make signature verification depend on HashMap iteration order. Reject duplicate logical signature headers before HMAC verification and add focused regressions.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:06:30.371970Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T23:06:33.616406Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","security","tests"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zp1xx","title":"[REVIEW-P4][MEDIUM] fcp-crypto::CwtClaims::constraints_cbor panics on invalid CBOR","description":"crates/fcp-crypto/src/cose.rs:223-235 documents that constraints_cbor takes pre-serialized CBOR, but it deserializes with ciborium::from_reader(cbor_bytes).expect("constraints_cbor: invalid CBOR"). Bug scenario: any caller that forwards malformed or attacker-supplied constraint bytes into this public builder path can abort the process instead of getting a typed CryptoError. This is especially dangerous because the rest of the COSE/CWT surface is fail-closed and error-returning, so a single malformed constraints blob becomes a panic denial-of-service instead of a normal token-construction failure. Suggested fix: make constraints_cbor return CryptoResult<Self> (or add a fallible companion and deprecate the panicking variant) and reject invalid CBOR without unwinding.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:00.235836Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:22.434941Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:22.432735Z","close_reason":"Added fallible CwtClaims::try_constraints_cbor + CapabilityTokenBuilder::try_constraints_cbor returning CryptoResult<Self> with CryptoError::SerializationError on invalid CBOR. The existing panic-prone constraints_cbor is now #[deprecated] AND no longer panics — it tracing-warns and leaves the constraints claim UNSET on malformed input, so a legacy caller's worst-case outcome is a token without constraints that the verifier's C3.4 constraints-required gate rejects at verify time, strictly better than a process abort. Added tracing as a production dep of fcp-crypto. 4 new regression tests (malformed CBOR rejection via try_ variant, valid CBOR acceptance, builder wrapper propagation, legacy shim no-panic behavior). Verified: 554 fcp-crypto lib tests pass (was 550 + 4 new).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zpihj","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-5: RevocationPushMessage two-tier signing transcript binding","description":"RevocationPushMessage has a documented two-tier signing model: (1) the zone OWNER signs the revocation content (owner_signing_bytes — zone_id + sorted revoked_ids + new_rev_seq, deliberately excluding from/timestamp so the owner sig is portable across forwarding peers), (2) the FORWARDING node signs the delivery envelope (signing_bytes — owner content PLUS from + timestamp). Today revocation_timing.rs constructs these messages but does not pin the transcript-binding properties. A regression that mixed from/timestamp into owner_signing_bytes would break the cross-peer portability that the design relies on; a regression that dropped from/timestamp from signing_bytes would let an attacker re-attribute a captured push. cc_2 domain (fcp-conformance + fcp-mesh).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:38:29.485466Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:40:41.678752Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:40:41.678226Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","mesh","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3003,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-zpihj","author":"jemanuel","text":"10b6dde9 adds revocation_push_signing_conformance.rs with 11 tests pinning the two-tier RevocationPushMessage transcript: owner-sig portability (from/timestamp excluded), owner-sig binding to zone_id/revoked_ids/new_rev_seq, node-sig binding to from/timestamp (forwarder attribution + replay defense), wrong-key rejection, revoked_ids order in owner transcript. All 11 pass in <0.01s. Note: commit also bundled unrelated qdrant connector files that another agent had staged but not committed; my conformance file is the intended payload.","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:40:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zt2h9","title":"fcp-streaming: make heartbeat, zombie, reconnect, and buffer constants env-overridable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:36.713051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:57.877386Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:57.877214Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","hardening","infra"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-zt2h9","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9fxph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:50.680420Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1952,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-zt2h9","author":"jemanuel","text":"AFFECTED FILES AND VALUES:
- src/lib.rs:48: DEFAULT_RECONNECT_DELAY = Duration::from_secs(1)
- src/lib.rs:51: MAX_RECONNECT_DELAY = Duration::from_secs(60)
- src/lib.rs:54: DEFAULT_BUFFER_SIZE = 8192
- src/health.rs:65: heartbeat_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30)
- src/health.rs:66: zombie_timeout: Duration::from_secs(120)
- src/health.rs:67: max_reconnect_attempts: 10
- src/websocket.rs:404-411: connect_timeout 30s, ping_interval 30s, pong_timeout 10s, max_message_size 64MB, reconnect_delay 1s, max_reconnect_attempts 10
- src/sse.rs:262-266: max_buffer_size 1MB, max_reconnect_attempts 10, reconnect_delay 1s

All of these are in Default::default() impls or module-level consts. They have builder/config struct overrides but no env-var path.

Steps:
1. For each StreamHealthConfig, WsConfig, SseConfig default: check env var before using compiled default
2. Env var naming convention: FCP_STREAMING_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_SECS, FCP_STREAMING_ZOMBIE_TIMEOUT_SECS, FCP_STREAMING_MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS, FCP_STREAMING_BUFFER_SIZE, etc.
3. Add parse-with-fallback helper: fn env_or<T: FromStr>(var: &str, default: T) -> T
4. Document env vars in module-level rustdoc
5. Keep all existing builder methods working (explicit config > env var > compiled default)
6. Add tests verifying env var override path","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:50Z"}]}
